facts.statement: The provisions of this code shall not be deemed to nullify any provisions of government law. 102.3 Application of references.
facts.source_quote: The provisions of this code shall not be deemed to nullify any provisions of government law. 102.3 Application of references.

facts.statement: Such policies and procedures shall not have the effect of waiving requirements specifically provided for in this code.
facts.source_quote: Such policies and procedures shall not have the effect of waiving requirements specifically provided for in this code.

facts.statement: Evaluation of testing and materials technology has permitted the development of new criteria that old materials may not satisfy.
facts.source_quote: Evaluation of testing and materials technology has permitted the development of new criteria that old materials may not satisfy.

facts.statement: The fact that a material, product or method of construction is not addressed in the code is not an indication that such material, product or method is intended to be prohibited.
facts.source_quote: The fact that a material, product or method of construction is not addressed in the code is not an indication that such material, product or method is intended to be prohibited.

facts.statement: Approval as a result of an inspection shall not be construed to be an approval of a violation of the provisions of this code or of other ordinances of the jurisdiction.
facts.source_quote: Approval as a result of an inspection shall not be construed to be an approval of a violation of the provisions of this code or of other ordinances of the jurisdiction.

facts.statement: Inspections presuming to give authority to violate or cancel the provisions of this code or of other ordinances of the jurisdiction shall not be valid.
facts.source_quote: Inspections presuming to give authority to violate or cancel the provisions of this code or of other ordinances of the jurisdiction shall not be valid.

facts.statement: A building or structure shall not be used or occupied, and a change in the existing use or occupancy classification of a building or structure or portion thereof shall not be made, until the building official has issued a certificate of occupancy therefor as - provided herein.
facts.source_quote: A building or structure shall not be used or occupied, and a change in the existing use or occupancy classification of a building or structure or portion thereof shall not be made, until the building official has issued a certificate of occupancy therefor as - provided herein.

facts.statement: Issuance of a certificate of occupancy shall not be construed as an approval of a violation of the provisions of this code or of other ordinances of the jurisdiction.
facts.source_quote: Issuance of a certificate of occupancy shall not be construed as an approval of a violation of the provisions of this code or of other ordinances of the jurisdiction.

facts.statement: The certification is intended to acknowledge that some building features may not be completed even though the building is safe for occupancy, or that a portion of the building can be safely occupied while work continues in another area.
facts.source_quote: The certification is intended to acknowledge that some building features may not be completed even though the building is safe for occupancy, or that a portion of the building can be safely occupied while work continues in another area.

facts.statement: However, it may or may not be deemed by the courts as representing a “good faith” effort to notify.
facts.source_quote: However, it may or may not be deemed by the courts as representing a “good faith” effort to notify.

facts.statement: The accessible means of egress requirements may not be the same route as that required for ingress into the building (see Sections 1104 and 1105).
facts.source_quote: The accessible means of egress requirements may not be the same route as that required for ingress into the building (see Sections 1104 and 1105).

facts.statement: The alarm verification feature may not retard signal activation for a period of more than 60 seconds and must not apply to alarm-initiating devices other than smoke detectors (which may be connected to the same circuit).
facts.source_quote: The alarm verification feature may not retard signal activation for a period of more than 60 seconds and must not apply to alarm-initiating devices other than smoke detectors (which may be connected to the same circuit).

facts.statement: Alarm verification is not the same as presignal features that delay an alarm signal for more than 1 minute and that are allowed only where specifically permitted by the building official.
facts.source_quote: Alarm verification is not the same as presignal features that delay an alarm signal for more than 1 minute and that are allowed only where specifically permitted by the building official.

facts.statement: A mercantile building is open to the public, many of whom may not be regular visitors.
facts.source_quote: A mercantile building is open to the public, many of whom may not be regular visitors.

facts.statement: A covered mall building and attached anchor buildings and parking garages shall be surrounded on all sides by a permanent open space or not less than 18 m.
facts.source_quote: A covered mall building and attached anchor buildings and parking garages shall be surrounded on all sides by a permanent open space or not less than 18 m.

facts.statement: An open mall building and anchor buildings and parking garages adjoining the perimeter line shall be surrounded on all sides by a permanent open space of not less than 18 m.
facts.source_quote: An open mall building and anchor buildings and parking garages adjoining the perimeter line shall be surrounded on all sides by a permanent open space of not less than 18 m.

facts.statement: The required minimum open space of 18 m must also be provided at any anchor buildings and parking garages attached to the covered mall building.
facts.source_quote: The required minimum open space of 18 m must also be provided at any anchor buildings and parking garages attached to the covered mall building.

facts.statement: Where permitted in Section 507, unlimited area buildings require an open space around the building of 18 m to prevent exposure fire spread.
facts.source_quote: Where permitted in Section 507, unlimited area buildings require an open space around the building of 18 m to prevent exposure fire spread.

facts.statement: This reduction is permitted in these locations where the exterior wall in the required space area is rated for not less than 3 hours and does not include more than 75 percent of the building perimeter.
facts.source_quote: This reduction is permitted in these locations where the exterior wall in the required space area is rated for not less than 3 hours and does not include more than 75 percent of the building perimeter.

facts.statement: Groups E, H, I and R occupancies are not prohibited from being within a covered or open mall building or an anchor store, but the exception would prohibit the use of reduced open space where one of these occupancies was a part of the covered or open mall building or anchor building. 402.3 Lease plan
facts.source_quote: Groups E, H, I and R occupancies are not prohibited from being within a covered or open mall building or an anchor store, but the exception would prohibit the use of reduced open space where one of these occupancies was a part of the covered or open mall building or anchor building. 402.3 Lease plan

facts.statement: The location of tenant separations may not be known until leases are negotiated with prospective tenants.
facts.source_quote: The location of tenant separations may not be known until leases are negotiated with prospective tenants.

facts.statement: The code requires that the construction documents be kept until the project is complete or for at least 5 years, whichever is longer. 104.6.1 Approvals.
facts.source_quote: The code requires that the construction documents be kept until the project is complete or for at least 5 years, whichever is longer. 104.6.1 Approvals.

facts.statement: Asphalt (tar) kettles shall not be transported over any highway, road or street when the heat source for the kettle is operating.
facts.source_quote: Asphalt (tar) kettles shall not be transported over any highway, road or street when the heat source for the kettle is operating.

facts.statement: Maintaining a fire under a kettle during transport is usually unnecessary and, therefore, prohibited, since little additional heat is required to return the asphalt to a usable consistency.
facts.source_quote: Maintaining a fire under a kettle during transport is usually unnecessary and, therefore, prohibited, since little additional heat is required to return the asphalt to a usable consistency.

facts.statement: The employee shall be within 30 m of the kettle and have the kettle within sight.
facts.source_quote: The employee shall be within 30 m of the kettle and have the kettle within sight.

facts.statement: # Section 304.1 ## Canonical Code Text 304.1 Waste accumulation prohibited.
facts.source_quote: # Section 304.1 ## Canonical Code Text 304.1 Waste accumulation prohibited.

facts.statement: # Section 307.1.1 ## Canonical Code Text 307.1.1 Prohibited open burning.
facts.source_quote: # Section 307.1.1 ## Canonical Code Text 307.1.1 Prohibited open burning.

facts.statement: Open burning shall be prohibited when atmospheric conditions or local circumstances make such fires hazardous. -
facts.source_quote: Open burning shall be prohibited when atmospheric conditions or local circumstances make such fires hazardous. -

facts.statement: See the commentary to Section 202for the definition of “Open burning” for a discussion of the types of burning intended to be regulated by this section. 307.1.1 Prohibited open burning.
facts.source_quote: See the commentary to Section 202for the definition of “Open burning” for a discussion of the types of burning intended to be regulated by this section. 307.1.1 Prohibited open burning.

facts.statement: The location for open burning shall be not less than 15 m from any structure, and provisions shall be made to prevent the fire from spreading to within 15 m of any structure.
facts.source_quote: The location for open burning shall be not less than 15 m from any structure, and provisions shall be made to prevent the fire from spreading to within 15 m of any structure.

facts.statement: Fires in approved containers that are not less than 4.5 m from a structure. 2.
facts.source_quote: Fires in approved containers that are not less than 4.5 m from a structure. 2.

facts.statement: The minimum required distance from a structure shall be 7.5 m where the pile size is
facts.source_quote: The minimum required distance from a structure shall be 7.5 m where the pile size is

facts.statement: # Section 310.4 ## Canonical Code Text 310.4 Removal of signs prohibited.
facts.source_quote: # Section 310.4 ## Canonical Code Text 310.4 Removal of signs prohibited.

facts.statement: Liquid or gas-fueled vehicles, boats or other motorcraft shall not be located indoors except as follows: 1.
facts.source_quote: Liquid or gas-fueled vehicles, boats or other motorcraft shall not be located indoors except as follows: 1.

facts.statement: Fuel in fuel tanks does not exceed one- quarter tank or 19 L (whichever is least). 3.
facts.source_quote: Fuel in fuel tanks does not exceed one- quarter tank or 19 L (whichever is least). 3.

facts.statement: In the event that any part or provision of this code is held to be illegal or void, this shall not have the effect of making void or illegal any of the other parts or provisions. 102.6 Existing structures.
facts.source_quote: In the event that any part or provision of this code is held to be illegal or void, this shall not have the effect of making void or illegal any of the other parts or provisions. 102.6 Existing structures.

facts.statement: The building official has the authority to extend this time limitation (in increments of 90 days), provided there is reasonable cause.
facts.source_quote: The building official has the authority to extend this time limitation (in increments of 90 days), provided there is reasonable cause.

facts.statement: The issuance or granting of a permit shall not be construed to be a permit for, or an approval of, any violation of any of the provisions of this code or of any other ordinance of the jurisdiction.
facts.source_quote: The issuance or granting of a permit shall not be construed to be a permit for, or an approval of, any violation of any of the provisions of this code or of any other ordinance of the jurisdiction.

facts.statement: Permits presuming to give authority to violate or cancel the provisions of this code or other ordinances of the jurisdiction shall not be valid.
facts.source_quote: Permits presuming to give authority to violate or cancel the provisions of this code or other ordinances of the jurisdiction shall not be valid.

facts.statement: The issuance of a permit based on construction documents and other data shall not prevent the building official from requiring the correction of errors in the construction documents and other data.
facts.source_quote: The issuance of a permit based on construction documents and other data shall not prevent the building official from requiring the correction of errors in the construction documents and other data.

facts.statement: Every permit issued shall become invalid unless the work on the site authorized by such permit is commenced within 180 days after its issuance, or if the work authorized on the site by such permit is suspended or abandoned for a period of 180 days after the time the work is commenced.
facts.source_quote: Every permit issued shall become invalid unless the work on the site authorized by such permit is commenced within 180 days after its issuance, or if the work authorized on the site by such permit is suspended or abandoned for a period of 180 days after the time the work is commenced.

facts.statement: The building official is authorized to grant, in writing, one or more extensions of time, for periods not more than 180 days each.
facts.source_quote: The building official is authorized to grant, in writing, one or more extensions of time, for periods not more than 180 days each.

facts.statement: The building official has the authority to extend this time limitation (in increments of 180 days), provided the extension is requested in writing and there is reason- able cause, which typically includes events beyond the permit holder’s control. 105.6 Suspension or revocation.
facts.source_quote: The building official has the authority to extend this time limitation (in increments of 180 days), provided the extension is requested in writing and there is reason- able cause, which typically includes events beyond the permit holder’s control. 105.6 Suspension or revocation.

facts.statement: An example of work that may not involve the submission of detailed construction documents is the replacement of an existing 60-amp electrical service with a 200-amp service.
facts.source_quote: An example of work that may not involve the submission of detailed construction documents is the replacement of an existing 60-amp electrical service with a 200-amp service.

facts.statement: Otherwise, a significant amendment may not be submitted because of misinterpretation, resulting in an activity that is not approved and that causes a needless delay in obtaining approval of the finished work. 107.5 Retention of construction documents.
facts.source_quote: Otherwise, a significant amendment may not be submitted because of misinterpretation, resulting in an activity that is not approved and that causes a needless delay in obtaining approval of the finished work. 107.5 Retention of construction documents.

facts.statement: One set of approved construction documents shall be retained by the building official for a period of not less than 180 days from date of completion of the permitted work except those subject to 104.7 .
facts.source_quote: One set of approved construction documents shall be retained by the building official for a period of not less than 180 days from date of completion of the permitted work except those subject to 104.7 .

facts.statement: Such permits shall be limited as to time of service but shall not be permitted for more than 180 days.
facts.source_quote: Such permits shall be limited as to time of service but shall not be permitted for more than 180 days.

facts.statement: If the occupant load of the building is less than 50 persons, the building could be classified as Group B (see Section 303.1.1).
facts.source_quote: If the occupant load of the building is less than 50 persons, the building could be classified as Group B (see Section 303.1.1).

facts.statement: Because of the multitiered design of most Group A-4 and A-5 occupancies, the limit for accessory occupancies to account for less than 10 percent of the story will need to be creatively applied.
facts.source_quote: Because of the multitiered design of most Group A-4 and A-5 occupancies, the limit for accessory occupancies to account for less than 10 percent of the story will need to be creatively applied.

facts.statement: Children 21/2 years of age or less usually are not able to recognize an emergency situation, may not respond appropriately or simply may not be able to egress without assistance.
facts.source_quote: Children 21/2 years of age or less usually are not able to recognize an emergency situation, may not respond appropriately or simply may not be able to egress without assistance.

facts.statement: Ninety-eight percent of households in the U.S. have less than 16 occupants, thus the limit of 16 is considered appropriate for a residential occupancy.
facts.source_quote: Ninety-eight percent of households in the U.S. have less than 16 occupants, thus the limit of 16 is considered appropriate for a residential occupancy.

facts.statement: Where a facility offers medical care instead of custodial care, it is assumed that residents may not be capable of self- preservation.
facts.source_quote: Where a facility offers medical care instead of custodial care, it is assumed that residents may not be capable of self- preservation.

facts.statement: The volume is based on the legacy code definition, which was based on 10,000 passenger vehicle tires weighing an average of 10 kg each, rather than the volume of the stored tires.
facts.source_quote: The volume is based on the legacy code definition, which was based on 10,000 passenger vehicle tires weighing an average of 10 kg each, rather than the volume of the stored tires.

facts.statement: Covered mall buildings greater than 4500 m2 in area and open mall buildings greater than 4500 m2 within the established perimeter line shall be provided with emergency power that is capable of operating the emergency voice/alarm communication system in accordance with Section 2702. -
facts.source_quote: Covered mall buildings greater than 4500 m2 in area and open mall buildings greater than 4500 m2 within the established perimeter line shall be provided with emergency power that is capable of operating the emergency voice/alarm communication system in accordance with Section 2702. -

facts.statement: In some instances, it may not be necessary to apply the provisions of Chapter 4.
facts.source_quote: In some instances, it may not be necessary to apply the provisions of Chapter 4.

facts.statement: The requirement for a minimum capacity of 1600 kg for fire service access elevators allows for design flexibility in the selection of elevator cars while still providing the necessary capacity and size for emergency responder needs.
facts.source_quote: The requirement for a minimum capacity of 1600 kg for fire service access elevators allows for design flexibility in the selection of elevator cars while still providing the necessary capacity and size for emergency responder needs.

facts.statement: Elevators are still prohibited by Section 1003.7 from serving as a means of egress for the general population of a building.
facts.source_quote: Elevators are still prohibited by Section 1003.7 from serving as a means of egress for the general population of a building.

facts.statement: However, in high-rise buildings in excess of 128 m in height, Section 403.5.2 requires there be an exit stairway provided in addition to those needed for egress capacity or travel distance purposes.
facts.source_quote: However, in high-rise buildings in excess of 128 m in height, Section 403.5.2 requires there be an exit stairway provided in addition to those needed for egress capacity or travel distance purposes.

facts.statement: The aggregate area of openings in a solid sleeping room face in Occupancy Conditions 2, 3, 4 and 5 shall not exceed 0.077 m2.
facts.source_quote: The aggregate area of openings in a solid sleeping room face in Occupancy Conditions 2, 3, 4 and 5 shall not exceed 0.077 m2.

facts.statement: Openings shall be not more than 900 mm above the floor.
facts.source_quote: Openings shall be not more than 900 mm above the floor.
